summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of StudioApp MVP. 29/29 pytest cases pass. Frontend E2E flow verified: super-admin login, tenant login, gallery creation, image upload with thumbnail generation, share link creation, and public ShareView with favourites/download-all all functional.

minor.issue: Pillow throws 'OSError: broken data stream' on very tiny (1x1) PNGs. The background thumb executor silently swallows the exception (except Exception: return False) so 'has_thumb' never flips. Not a real-world issue (real photos > 100px work fine) but suggest logging the exception so failed derivatives are debuggable.
Media served publicly by gallery_id UUID with no auth (intentional per handoff notes for MVP). Should be revisited before production because knowing/guessing UUID leaks originals cross-tenant.
'pending_albums' counts galleries with 0 files in 'Album Favourites'. Newly-created galleries always count as pending. Cosmetic only.

issues: Large stat digits (STUDIOS=1, ACTIVE=1) render with a very tall/narrow serif '1' that looks slightly clipped on top on some viewports

action_items: Consider logging exceptions in media.generate_image_derivatives instead of silently returning False
For prod: gate /api/media/* endpoints behind share-token or admin-auth (currently public by gallery_id UUID — intentional MVP)
critical_code_review_comments: auth_utils._clean should also strip password_hash from tenant docs returned to super admin (list_tenants returns tenants but tenants collection has no password_hash so ok — admins do)
server.py CORS uses allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True which browsers reject silently. If cookie auth is added later, use explicit origins
routes/galleries.py _thumb_and_mark opens a fresh MongoClient per file; not pooled — fine for MVP but wasteful

public_share.share_download does not verify password for password-protected shares before download (only /access does). A client that knows the file_id + token can bypass the password gate for downloads. HIGH concern for password-protected galleries
shares.py does not validate access_level against the allowed set ('view'|'download'|'full') — any string accepted
seed_data_creation: During tests: several 'TEST Gallery ...' galleries + one 'E2E Test Gallery' on demo tenant with a 400x300 PNG. A share token 'VOPtf91E4RvtiA' remains for future E2E frontend re-testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, parallel-safe). Run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. E2E share URL for frontend re-test: https://<host>/s/VOPtf91E4RvtiA (from demo tenant, no password, download-enabled).
rca of the issue: Only issue encountered was initial test failure on tiny 1x1 PNG upload — Pillow's decoder fails on it and the background thumb generator caught the exception (silent). Fixed test to use a 100x100 PNG. Root-cause suggestion for E1: log the exception in media.generate_image_derivatives so silent-fail edge cases (corrupt / tiny images) surface in logs.
